# Accordion

A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that reveal sections of content.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Selection behavior](#selection-behavior)
- [Accessibility and content](#accessibility-and-content)
- [Animation](#animation)

## Installation

Add the Accordion component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/accordion
```

## Usage

Use an accordion to organize related sections when showing every section at once would make the page difficult to scan. Each `AccordionItem` needs a stable, unique `value` so the root can track which content is open.

```vue
<template>
  <Accordion
    type="single"
    class="w-full max-w-xl"
    collapsible
    :default-value="defaultValue"
  >
    <AccordionItem
      v-for="item in accordionItems"
      :key="item.value"
      :value="item.value"
    >
      <AccordionTrigger>{{ item.title }}</AccordionTrigger>
      <AccordionContent>
        {{ item.content }}
      </AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
  </Accordion>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionContent,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Accordion'

const defaultValue = 'item-1'

const accordionItems = [
  {
    value: 'item-1',
    title: 'Is it accessible?',
    content: 'Yes. It follows the WAI-ARIA accordion design pattern.',
  },
  {
    value: 'item-2',
    title: 'Can it be collapsed?',
    content: 'Yes. Use the collapsible prop with a single-value accordion.',
  },
  {
    value: 'item-3',
    title: 'Can it be animated?',
    content: 'Yes. The content uses the accordion animation utilities.',
  },
]
</script>
```

## Selection behavior

Set `type="single"` when the sections are alternatives and only one should remain open. Add `collapsible` when closing the current item to leave every section closed is useful. Use `type="multiple"` when people may need to compare content across sections.

Use `default-value` for an initially open uncontrolled item. Bind `v-model` when another part of the interface needs to read or change the selection. A single accordion models a string; a multiple accordion models an array of item values.

## Accessibility and content

Keep every trigger concise and descriptive: it becomes the interactive heading for its panel. The primitives connect triggers and content and expose expanded state to assistive technology. Do not hide information that must be discovered immediately, validation errors, or the only way to complete the current task inside a closed item.

`AccordionTrigger` accepts the primitive composition props. When using `as-child`, preserve a focusable control and read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before replacing the default trigger element.

## Animation

Accordion content uses Tailwind theme tokens for its open and close animations. Add these tokens to your application stylesheet if they are not already present.

*resources/css/app.css*

```css
@theme inline {
  --animate-accordion-down: accordion-down 0.2s ease-out;
  --animate-accordion-up: accordion-up 0.2s ease-out;

  @keyframes accordion-down {
    from { height: 0; }
    to { height: var(--reka-accordion-content-height); }
  }

  @keyframes accordion-up {
    from { height: var(--reka-accordion-content-height); }
    to { height: 0; }
  }
}
```

Hosted documentation: [Accordion](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/accordion)
